snippets.title: Create, add, remove, test
snippets.code:
tools = {"web_search", "code_interp"}

tools.add("web_search")     # duplicate: no-op
tools.add("image_gen")       # {'web_search','code_interp','image_gen'}

tools.discard("none")        # safe remove (no KeyError)
tools.remove("image_gen")    # KeyError if missing

print("web_search" in tools) # True   - O(1)
print(len(tools))
snippets.explanation: Adds are idempotent; use discard for safe removal.

snippets.title: Dedupe a list (preserving order)
snippets.code:
ids = [3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 5]

# Order-preserving dedupe with a set as a memory:
seen = set()
unique = []
for i in ids:
    if i not in seen:
        seen.add(i)
        unique.append(i)
print(unique)   # [3, 1, 4, 5, 9, 2, 6]

# Order-INSENSITIVE one-liner:
unique_unordered = list(set(ids))
snippets.explanation: `list(set(xs))` drops duplicates but loses order; use a seen-set when order matters.

snippets.title: Set algebra
snippets.explanation: Intersection enforces an allow-list in one expression.

snippets.title: frozenset as a dict key
snippets.code:
# Cache a routing decision keyed by an unordered set of features.
key   = frozenset({"has_image", "long_context"})
routes = {
    frozenset({"has_image"}): "vision-model",
    frozenset({"has_image", "long_context"}): "vision-long-ctx",
}
print(routes[key])   # 'vision-long-ctx'
snippets.explanation: frozenset is hashable, so it can sit inside another set or be a dict key.
